# branch onto a branch that has a prior restack merge commit
# should not carry over old parent's changes to the new parent.
#
# Regression test: when using merge restack method,
# a branch that was restacked onto its parent (creating a merge commit)
# should not retain the old parent's changes after 'gs branch onto'.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2026-03-13T10:00:00Z'

mkdir repo
cd repo
git init
git add init.txt
git commit -m 'Initial commit'
gs repo init

# Configure merge-based restacking
git config spice.restack.method merge

# Create feat1 and child on top of it
git add feat1.txt
gs bc feat1 -m feat1

gs branch checkout feat1
git add child.txt
gs bc child -m child

# Add more commits to feat1 (simulating feat1 getting new work)
gs branch checkout feat1
git add feat1b.txt
gs cc -m 'feat1: add more'

# gs cc automatically restacks upstack branches,
# so child is already restacked onto the updated feat1
# (with a merge commit bringing in feat1b.txt).
gs branch checkout child
exists feat1b.txt

# Create feat2 as an independent branch from trunk
gs trunk
git add feat2.txt
gs bc feat2 -m feat2

# Now move child onto feat2.
# child's history contains a merge commit from feat1,
# but after the onto operation, feat1's changes must not be present.
gs branch checkout child
gs branch onto feat2
stderr 'child: moved onto feat2'

# feat1's changes must NOT be present in child's worktree
! exists feat1.txt
! exists feat1b.txt

# feat2 and child's own changes must be present
exists feat2.txt
exists child.txt

-- repo/init.txt --
initial
-- repo/feat1.txt --
feature 1
-- repo/feat1b.txt --
feature 1b
-- repo/feat2.txt --
feature 2
-- repo/child.txt --
child feature
